panoramas.title: Manoa Garden Panorama
panoramas.description: Lush and green, this view is super accessible and leads to the closest waterfall to Waikīkī, Mānoa Falls. The hike is easy and the scenery is amazing. Tucked back into a dense, green jungle that looks like something out of a Jurassic Park movie, its an adventure you won't want to miss.

panoramas.title: Peeking Gecko Pano
panoramas.description: A bright green Day Gecko hiding in some orange foliage. A beautiful day in Mānoa Valley,  off and on showers and some dense foliage, create the ideal hiding place for this colorful creature.

panoramas.title: Tropical City Pano
panoramas.description: The view from the top of Diamond Head Crater is incredible. Sweeping views of the city of  Honolulu and the famous Waikīkī coastline with its iconic Pink Hotel and world-renowned surf breaks.

panoramas.title: Mauna Loa Pano
panoramas.description: Mauna Loa is the world's largest active volcano, located on the Big Island of Hawaiʻi, the name means long mountain. It has erupted only once in the last 40 years. This is the perfect view seen from Mauna Kea at sunset.

panoramas.title: Rabbit Island Sunrise Pano
panoramas.description: A sunrise from a tidepool near Makapu'u beach with Mānana Island, aka Rabbit Island, in the distance.  The Island once had rabbits but is now a protected bird sanctuary.

panoramas.title: Waimea Sunset Silhouette Pano
panoramas.description: A sunset, Waimea Bay, and plam trees. After a long day swimming in the warm, calm waters of the bay, sit back and relax while the sun sets over the water and palm trees sway in the breeze.

panoramas.title: Nighttime Fuji Pano
panoramas.description: Mt Fuji under a blanket of stars. Yamanakako lake reflecting a perfect Fujisan, with a bright moon and a sky full of stars. The nights are calm and so peaceful, enjoying this scene on a winter night in Japan.

panoramas.title: Clownfish Pano
panoramas.description: The sealife in Japan is incredible and completely different form what you see in Hawaiʻi. One of the most popular fish, the clownfish, is abundant in Japanese waters. There are so many types and they are all different sizes, but one thing is consistent, you wonʻt find them far from an anemone.

panoramas.title: Kualoa Reflections Pano
panoramas.description: On a perfect day, one of the most unmistakable views of the majestic Koʻolau Mountains, can be seen from across the Kāneʻohe Bay on Oʻahu. Home to Jurassic park and some incredible views, it was once one of the most sacred places on island. You can also see the small island nicknamed Chinamanʻs Hat, just off the coast.

panoramas.title: Fall Fuji Pano
panoramas.description: Japan is gorgeous during the fall season. Crisp air, snow capped volcanoes and red and golden leaves a plenty. Mount Fuji is amazing from almost any angle and especially nestled between some perfect fall foliage.

panoramas.title: Water Lily Pano
panoramas.description: The Blue Tropical Water Lily. Beautiful, detailed, colorful and sparkling with rain drops while surrpunded by sparkling, green lily pads. Caught it opening up just as the sun peeked out from behind the clouds!

panoramas.title: Hidden Gecko Pano
panoramas.description: I am not sure if there is such a thing as too many geckos. One of my favorite critters in Hawaiʻi has to be the colorful Day Gecko. They are found hiding in dense foliage, colorful flowers and even on random buildings in town. You never know when or where you will encounter one.

panoramas.title: Heliconia Pano
panoramas.description: The lush, dense forests on Oʻahu are full of tropical flora and fauna. One of my favorite plants is the Heliconia. This is perfection with the light shining down, through the canopy, hitting this flower in just the right spot to make it glow.

aerial.title: Makapuʻu II
aerial.description: An aerial view of a secluded, scenic beach in Hawaiʻi. Watching the waves crash over the rocks is one of the most comforting and relaxing spectacles from the sky.

aerial.title: Tropical Ballin'
aerial.description: An empty basketball court surrounded by lush green grass and swaying palm trees. If this is not an athletes dream then I don't know what is. Early morning light just after a light rain and crisp winter air make for a nice morning to shoot some hoops.

aerial.title: The Cove
aerial.description: Oʻahu has plenty of nice beaches and some of them are more secluded than others. This beach is well-known but also kind of hidden. Featured in tv shows and movies, the Hālona Beach Cove seen from the sky at sunrise. Nice and empty before all the tour buses arrive.

aerial.title: Hilton Beach
aerial.description: Waikīkī Beach from the Hilton Hawaiian Village. A busy, sunny Thanksgiving day, perfect for a day at the beach!

aerial.title: The Cove II
aerial.description: Oʻahu has plenty of nice beaches and some of them are more secluded than others. This beach is well-known but also kind of hidden. Featured in tv shows and movies, the Hālona Beach Cove seen from the sky at sunrise. Nice and empty before all the tour buses arrive.

aerial.title: Walls (Horizontal)
aerial.description: The famous rock cement pier at Waikīkī Beach. During the summer you'll find kids jumping and body boarders riding waves. On the right it creates a perfect wading pool for families and kids to cool off under the hot Hawaiian sun. If you go early it will be empty and as the day goes on it becomes the most crowded part of the beach. In a vertical format.

aerial.title: Walls (Vertical)
aerial.description: The famous rock cement pier at Waikīkī Beach. During the summer you'll find kids jumping and body boarders riding waves. On the right it creates a perfect wading pool for families and kids to cool off under the hot Hawaiian sun. If you go early it will be empty and as the day goes on it becomes the most crowded part of the beach. In a horizontal format.

standard.title: Milky Makapuʻu
standard.description: Oʻahu is very populated and not known for having dark skies, but if you go to the right spot on the right night things can line up. Makapuʻu is an amazing beach by day and a solid spot for stargazing on a warm summer night

standard.title: Rabbit Island Milky Way
standard.description: Rabbit Island, where the galaxy begins or ends. Time slows down when you stare up into the universe. Make sure to spend at least a couple nights each summer out in the wild night, gazing into the stars, contemplating life and getting some perspective.

standard.title: Nighttime Fuji
standard.description: Mt Fuji under a blanket of stars. Yamanakako lake reflecting a perfect Fujisan, with a bright moon and a sky full of stars. It is cold but so peaceful, enjoying this scene on a winter night in Japan.

standard.title: Ray
standard.description: Crystal clear water and a solo spotted eagle ray, hovering just above the sand, in search of food. This one kept burying its nose in the sand while looking for something to eat. They have such amazing spots with so much intricate detail, it is hard to look away.

standard.title: Hidden Honu
standard.description: A quiet Hawaiian Honu rests on the ocean floor, hiding behind a wall of yellow snappers. The Hawaiian green sea turtle is one of the most iconic sea creatures in Hawaiʻi.

standard.title: Morning Commute
standard.description: When I first moved to Oʻahu, I did not have a car, so I had to take the bus to go on adventures. This day, I made the long trip out west to go for a snorkel and I was rewarded with a perfect, crystal clear day. On top of that, there were so many dolphins that came close to shore that I could feel the traffic underwater. I got there just in time for rush hour and it did not disappoint

standard.title: Seis Rays
standard.description: Six spotted eagle rays gracefully gliding above the ocean floor. They can be found in deep waters and shallow waters, in large groups called fevers.

standard.title: Cuatro Rays
standard.description: Spotted Eagle Rays, fascinating, graceful, majestic and shy. The details of their patterns and the grace with which they swim is something to admire. They are such amazing creatures and my favorite to see when in the water.

standard.title: Deep Teal
standard.description: The ocean is life, it is power, it is home. There is nothing like riding a wave and experiencing the ocean's power. Getting caught inside a barrel is the dream. Put yourself right there, in the perfect spot and enjoy the water breaking just over your head!

standard.title: Sandy Textures
standard.description: While most people go snorkeling to see fish and wild life, others notice details, textures and patterns that many miss. There is something really fascinating about the sea floor, how the sand moves and how the swells and nature shape it.

standard.title: Clowning Around
standard.description: Nothing like exploring the waters of a new country. Japan has Okinawa, which is nicknamed the "Hawaiʻi" of Japan, and it does not disappoint. There are so many varieties of the iconic clownfish everywhere.

standard.title: Double Trouble
standard.description: The ocean in Okinawa Japan is incredible, with its crystal clear, water and warm temperatures, it has been nicknamed the "Hawaiʻi of Japan". One thing you cannot find in the Hawaiian islands are clownfish. These two peeking out of their purple anemone to say hi.

standard.title: Honu Life
standard.description: A Hawaiian Green Sea Turtle floats magically just under the surface. They can be seen all over the Hawaiian island chain and are one of the most iconic sea creatures to boot.

standard.title: Underwater Butterflies
standard.description: There are so many colorful fish to see underwater and luckily, Hawaiian waters are some of the best in the world for snorkeling and exploring. This school of bright, yellow butterfly fish is no exception.

standard.title: Pod Squad
standard.description: Hawaiian Spinner Dolphins are super social creatures, swimming, hunting and eating in pods, not only for protection, but for fun. They rest during the day and can be seen traveling in small pods of 10-30 on a normal day.

standard.title: Flying Honu
standard.description: Hawaiian Green Sea Turtles can hold their breath for hours when sleeping or relaxing, but usually when they are active they surface much more frequently. Here a Honu is coming up for a quick breath before diving back down to hang out on the sandy ocean floor. It almost appears as if she is flying, using her front flippers as wings.

standard.title: Deep Blue
standard.description: A Galapagos Shark casually cruises by. Out in the deeper water, you never know what will come up to greet you. Being able to get up close and personal with a few sharks is a humbling experience, mixed with fear and respect, it is something special and inspiring.

standard.title: Gliding Rays
standard.description: One of my favorite sea creatures has got to be the Spotted Eagle Ray. The way they effortlessly glide through the water, in formation, is so awesome to witness. They glide and turn and travel all in unison like it's a performance!

standard.title: Mom & Baby
standard.description: Humpback whales migrate thousands of miles each year in order to give birth and raise their young. In Tahiti an incredible encounter with a mother and her calf was something I never thought that I would get to experience and is one thing that will stick with me forever!

standard.title: Hovering Honu
standard.description: A Hawaiian Green Sea Turtle hovers just above some very shallow reef on a very calm north shore day. Sharks cove during the summer is one of the best spots on island to spend a day snorkeling and relaxing.

standard.title: Pod Squad II
standard.description: A mellow pod of Hawaiian Spinner Dolphins cruising in crystal clear Hawaiian waters. Another underwater icon of Hawaiʻi that is so fun to see while snorkeling or standing on the shore.

standard.title: Surgeon School
standard.description: School is in session with this huge school of surgeon fish. It is very common to see these guys cruising around the warm, crystal clear water at any time if the day.

standard.title: Spring Train
standard.description: While walking along a path lined with cherry trees, I saw a distant train pass through the scene. The spring cherry blossoms perfectly framing the train looked so magical. I had to stop. I was standing there for the next 30 minutes, waiting patiently, for another train to pass through the right spot.

standard.title: Boats & Blossoms
standard.description: Tokyo has so many cherry blossoms, sakura in Japanese, its incredible. They line city streets and parks and rivers and every part of the area. I caught this boat cruising along a river, floating peacefully under lines and lines of gorgeous sakura.

standard.title: Hidden Sakura Temple
standard.description: Itsukushima Island is a popular tourist destination in Hiroshima Japan. Most people go to see the floating torii and then leave, but if you spend some time exploring the small island you will be delighted by the temples and cherry blossoms that abound.

standard.title: Island Torii
standard.description: On a small island, just off the coast of Hiroshima, sits an icon, the Itsukushima Shrine. This place is known for the famous "floating torii" when, at high tide, the water hides the base, making it appear as if it were floating out at sea. If you travel Itsukushima Island you will find beautiful cherry blossoms and empty stairways leading around a small village.

standard.title: Itsukushima Torii Blossoms
standard.description: Itsukushima Island, home of the famous "floating torii". It is usually viewed from down at waters edge, but if you hike around the island, there are many other amazing perspectives. Spring cherry blossoms surrounding the shrine was another Japan dream come true.

standard.title: Honcho Street Fuji
standard.description: There are so many famous spots in Japan that it is hard to count. Honcho Street is known for its view of Mount Fuji at the end. A cloudy, snow-capped Fujisan is all I needed to be happy.

standard.title: Kyoto Boat Boys
standard.description: Fall in Kyoto Japan may be the best time of year to visit. Take a ride on a traditional style Japanese boat up a river lined with maple trees. The guys use long bamboo poles to glide up and down the river.

standard.title: Hidden Momiji Temple
standard.description: A small shrine area tucked away, hidden in the trees. It offers the perfect glimpse of Japan in the fall with its mind blowing color and red maple leaves.

standard.title: Tokyo Taxi Driver
standard.description: Tokyo is so big and so densely populated that many residents do not drive, you have tons of public transportation. The number of taxi drivers in the city is outrageous.

standard.title: Kabukichō Closeup
standard.description: One of the most popular and lively districts in Tokyo, Kabukichō, where gates made of red lights glow so bright you cannot look away. These are some of the most famous gates in all of Japan. This close up get you nice and person with the famous red gate.

standard.title: Chicago Blues
standard.description: Right when we got to this train station, I knew that I was going to get one of my favorite shots of the trip. Chicago has so many stations and each one is unique. The wavy roof and cool blue tones created a really nice ambiance with the lines to framing the train and a few awaiting passengers.

standard.title: The Red Line
standard.description: One of my favorite cities to travel to is Chicago. Its such a cool city and the train system makes it so easy to get around. Its above ground tracks and below ground tunnels offer some very interesting photo opportunities.

standard.title: Torii Tunnel
standard.description: Japan has so many popular spots and I think Fushimi Inarai has got to be the top destination in Kyoto. The orange-red gates wind up the mountain, leading endlessly to a serene shrine. The higher up you get, the fewer people you encounter.

standard.title: Fuji and the Moon
standard.description: Clear skies, a snow-capped volcano, crisp fall air, and a crescent moon directly above the peak of Mount Fuji, what more could you ask for?

standard.title: Fuji Love (Vertical)
standard.description: Japan is a bucket list destination and a once in a lifetime trip for most. My first time there I knew that it was going to be more than just a one trip destination. Fall is one of the most amazing seasons to experience, the bright red maple leaves, golden ginkgo leaves Mt Fuji make fall the best time. Fujisan engulfed in the boldest, most intense red maple leaves I only imagined in my dreams! (In a vertical orientation)

standard.title: Fall Time Fuji
standard.description: Japan is gorgeous during the fall season. Crisp air, snow capped volcanoes and red and golden leaves a plenty. Mount Fuji is amazing from almost any angle and especially nestled between some perfect fall foliage.

standard.title: Fuji Love (Horizontal)
standard.description: Japan is a bucket list destination and a once in a lifetime trip for most. My first time there I knew that it was going to be more than just a one trip destination. Fall is one of the most amazing seasons to experience, the bright red maple leaves, golden ginkgo leaves Mt Fuji make fall the best time. Fujisan engulfed in the boldest, most intense red maple leaves I only imagined in my dreams! (In a horizontal orientation)

standard.title: Staring Contest
standard.description: If I lived in Nagano Japan, I would probably spend a lot of time in the hot springs. This Japanese Macaque or Snow Monkey saw me taking his photo and stared me down. I got the impression that I was interrupting his quiet time so I snapped the shot and let him return to relaxing in peace.

standard.title: Hidden Snow Monkey
standard.description: Japanese Macaques spend a lot of time in and around these natural hot springs in Nagano Japan. There is nothing better than relaxing in the springs without a care in the world. I wonder what he was thinking about and what he was looking at.

standard.title: Baby Macaque
standard.description: A cute little baby Japanese Macaque, just as amazed by all the humans as we were of it. This guy was hanging out, close to mom and near the onsen, as we waited for the snow to start falling.

standard.title: Snow Monkey Mama
standard.description: On a chilly fall day in Nagano Japan, a baby Japanese Macaque, affectionately known as a "Snow Monkey", chills in a onsen on its moms back. They are both enjoying its warm waters and escaping the frigid air just before a light snowfall.

standard.title: Fall Peak Fuji
standard.description: Japan has every season and they all shine. Fall is no exception. It is cool and the air is crisp and the maple leaves show their signature color before hibernating for the winter. Seeing Mt Fuji capped in snow with a beautiful, bright red maple tree draping over it is something words cannot describe.

standard.title: Peaky
standard.description: Simple, clean and moody. Clouds sitting just behind a beautiful, lush, green peak in the Koʻolau Mountain range.

standard.title: Sunset Simplicity
standard.description: It does not get any better than a classic sunset on the water. The sun slowly going down, disappering into the horizon as the sky lights up and the ocean gets darker. Perfection!

standard.title: Rabbit Island Sunrise
standard.description: A sunrise from a tidepool near Makapu'u beach with Manana Island, aka Rabbit Island, in the distance.  The Island once had rabbits but is now a protected bird sanctuary.

standard.title: Jurassic Mānoa
standard.description: Mānoa Falls is a popular hike for locals and tourists alike. It is full of dense jungle and bamboo. The entrance is like a gateway to another world or another time, as if leading to some place Jurassic.

standard.title: Heavenly Views
standard.description: The most dangerous and infamous hike on Oʻahu! Worth all the risks for this insane view.

standard.title: Nā Pali Ridges
standard.description: A close up and personal view of the ridges of the Nā Pali coastline. Kauaʻi has the best views.

standard.title: Rainbow (ānuenue)
standard.description: A Rainbow, close up and personal! A classic and magical Hawaiian phenomenon.

standard.title: Kāhala Fire
standard.description: A fire sunrise from a quiet and secluded neighborhood. The mornings bring many surprises when you wake up early.

standard.title: Purple Haze
standard.description: Tucked away in the corner of a small neighborhood sits a quiet little beach spot. Great for sunrise and some peaceful meditation. The perfect way to reset and prepare for the day.

standard.title: Waimea Ridges (Vertical)
standard.description: A close up view of the red and green ridges that are the Waimea Canyon. This canyon has earned the nickname, The Grand Canyon of Hawaiʻi, because of how grand and expansive it is. This place is insane to see with your own eyes

standard.title: Hawaiian Grand Canyon
standard.description: On the west side of Kauaʻi there is a massive canyon know as the, "Grand Canyon of the Pacific". Its gorgeous red and green ridges are the perfect contrasting  colors after a nice drive along a winding road.

standard.title: Nā Pali Streams
standard.description: Kauaʻi has so many incredible hikes and views, making it my favorite spot for outoor activities. You end up on top of the world gazing down at lush, green valleys below.

standard.title: Waimea Ridges (Horizontal)
standard.description: A close up view of the red and green ridges that are the Waimea Canyon. This canyon has earned the nickname, The Grand Canyon of Hawaiʻi, because of how grand and expansive it is. This place is insane to see with your own eyes.

standard.title: Mānoa Tree Garden
standard.description: Just as you begin , Mānoa Falls trail, you are greeted with an incredible view of dense green trees. It looks like something straight out of a Jurassic Park scene.

standard.title: Tahitian Bay Views
standard.description: On a peaceful little Tahitian island sits on of the most picturesque bays. Views of the mountains and the ocean make it a great spot to wake up early and start your day.

standard.title: Waimea Palms Sunset
standard.description: A nice sunset at Waimea Bay. The rocks and the palms silhouetted against a purple and orange dream.

standard.title: Sunset at the Bay
standard.description: A moody sunset falls over the famous Waimea Bay on Oʻahu. As the light fades, a few brave souls standing silhouetted on top of "The Rock" waiting and contemplating their final jump.

standard.title: Rugged Koko Coastline (Horizontal)
standard.description: Koko Head is an iconic, well known hike on Oʻahu. From the front you can see the 1000+ steps that makes the hike difficult for most. This view is the back side seen from one of the many rugged coastlines.

standard.title: Rugged Koko Coastline (Vertical)
standard.description: Hawaiʻi, a land full of rugged coastlines and volcanic craters. A view of the backside  of Koko Head Crater from a rugged beach.

standard.title: Sunrise Overload
standard.description: Sometimes the sky just absolutely explodes with light and color. This sunrise at Kāhala Beach was the most insane and perfect explosion of color that I have ever seen. It hurts to get up before the sun, but on occasion it is very worth it.

standard.title: Koko Crater Views (Vertical)
standard.description: The iconic Koko Head Crater viewed from an abandoned pillbox. Most people only recognize it from the front with its 1000+ stairs leading to the summit, but from behind you can really see the size and the shape. I like to imagine it erupting and enjoying that show would be incredible from this viewpoint. (In a vertical orientation)

standard.title: The Stairs
standard.description: Dangerous, Infamous, Iconic! The only way to Heaven.

standard.title: Koko Crater Views (Horizontal)
standard.description: The iconic Koko Head Crater viewed from an abandoned pillbox. Most people only recognize it from the front with its 1000+ stairs leading to the summit, but from behind you can really see the size and the shape. I like to imagine it erupting and enjoying that show would be incredible from this viewpoint. (In a horizontal orientation)

standard.title: Heaven's Stairs
standard.description: There are a lot of places in Hawaiʻi that feel like heaven. This view is definitely one of them. Its a long, steep, dangerous hike with other risks involved, but sometimes you just gotta live life on the edge.

standard.title: Olomana  Sunrise (Horizontal)
standard.description: A perfect golden morning view of the third peak. Considered one of the most dangerous legal hikes on Oʻahu, Olomana Trail also known as Three Peaks. The third and final peak is the most treacherous and steep climb of them all. (A horizontal orientation)

standard.title: Olomana  Sunrise (Vertical)
standard.description: A perfect golden morning view of the third peak. Considered one of the most dangerous legal hikes on Oʻahu, Olomana Trail also known as Three Peaks. The third and final peak is the most treacherous and steep climb of them all. (A vertical orientation)

standard.title: Glowing Ridges
standard.description: Morning light breaks through the clouds and shines on a small part of the Koʻolau Mountain Range. The range is home to some of the tallest peaks on Oʻahu, accessed via long ridge trails leading to insane views.

standard.title: San Diego Sunset Dreams
standard.description: Coastal cities are the best. I could not imagine living anywhere far away from the ocean. In San Diego I was blessed with one of the most incredible sunsets over La Jolla and Scripps pier.

standard.title: Seal Beach (Horizontal)
standard.description: Hawaiʻi has so much wildlife and a lot of it is protected. Seeing a Hawaiian Monk Seal is rare enough, but seeing three at the same time has only happened once in my time here. Throw in a golden sunset, glowing green palm trees and crystal clear water and you have a recipe for something incredible! The awesome, unexpected wildlife moments make Hawaiʻi  extra special to me. (In a horizontal orientation)

standard.title: Seal Beach (Vertical)
standard.description: Hawaiʻi has so much wildlife and a lot of it is protected. Seeing a Hawaiian Monk Seal is rare enough, but seeing three at the same time has only happened once in my time here. Throw in a golden sunset, glowing green palm trees and crystal clear water and you have a recipe for something incredible! The awesome, unexpected wildlife moments make Hawaiʻi  extra special to me. (In a vertical orientation)

standard.title: Palm Lagoon
standard.description: Tahiti holds a special place in my heart. It is more natural and more green and more wild than any place I have been. I imagine this is how Hawaiʻi once was, before it was discovered.

standard.title: Tohivea
standard.description: Tohivea. My favorite peak in Tahiti on the island of Moʻorea. It can be seen from all over the island and stand tall with its unique form and rugged lines. Clouds, morning light and sea birds make it a sight to see.

standard.title: Tahitian Peak Palms
standard.description: The island of Moʻorea has so many awesome peaks and is littered in palm trees and coconut trees. This huge tree lined up perfectly with one of the many rugged peaks.

standard.title: Salty Palms
standard.description: Palm trees that hang over the water and dip into the ocean on lower tides. These are some lucky trees.

standard.title: Tropical City (Vertical)
standard.description: Waikīkī and Honolulu viewed from the top of the Diamond Head Crater Trail. From here it looks more like a bustling city than a tropical island, but that is the allure of Oʻahu. The pink hotel stands at the center of Waikīkī Beach, surrounded by towering hotels and the most picturesque water you have ever seen. (A vertical orietnation)

standard.title: Tropical City (Horizontal)
standard.description: Waikīkī and Honolulu viewed from the top of the Diamond Head Crater Trail. From here it looks more like a bustling city than a tropical island, but that is the allure of Oʻahu. The pink hotel stands at the center of Waikīkī Beach, surrounded by towering hotels and the most picturesque water you have ever seen. (A horizontal orientation)

standard.title: Cham
standard.description: One of the lesser known critters in Hawaiʻi is the chameleon. They are hard to find and popular as pets. Such a unique creature, able to change colors and move its eyes independently. Found on a trail while hiking on Oʻahu.

standard.title: Happy Gecko
standard.description: A bright green Gold Dust Day Gecko, smiling at the camera, posing for its portrait and ready to be admired by everyone.

standard.title: Goldie
standard.description: A mellow Gold Dust Day Gecko, chilling and fearless, soaking up some midday sun. It does not get much closer than this. The detail of the blue eyes, red stripes on its nose and the blue feet are awesome.

standard.title: Hanging Hibiscus
standard.description: This was the first time that I had ever seen a Hibiscus hanging down like that. It caught my eye immediately and I could not help but admire it and snap a photo. It is known as the Japanese Lantern Hibiscus!

standard.title: Brownie
standard.description: The brown anole. They are everywhere!

standard.title: Pineapple Crown
standard.description: The most iconic fruit in Hawaiʻi, the pineapple. There are so many varieties with so many different colors, but the spiky crown on top stays constant.

standard.title: King of Kauaʻi
standard.description: Synonymous with Hawaiʻi, and especially Kauaʻi, is the chicken. They can be found pretty much everywhere walking around, strutting their stuff like they own the place. This king showed up ready for his debut.

standard.title: Heliconia Fire (Vertical)
standard.description: A Parrot Heliconia tucked away in some dense green foliage. It stands tall and strong, showing off its bright flower.

standard.title: Heliconia Fire (Horizontal)
standard.description: Another of the beautiful Heliconia family, the Parrot Heliconia, stand upright with its bright orange flowers and deep green leaves.

standard.title: Peaceful Lily
standard.description: While on a hike on Oʻahu, I saw the tallest, most perfectly white flower. The light hitting it just right in the dark, moody forest. Looks like a variety of the Peace Lily.

standard.title: Peaceful Gecko
standard.description: Geckos are everywhere in Hawaiʻi if you know where to look. Their bright green complexion can be hard to spot when laying on a bright green leaf of similar color. This little guy is just enjoying some Hawaiian sun after a short spring shower.

standard.title: Peek-a-Boo Gecko
standard.description: The ever so popular Gold Dust Day Gecko, while bright and colorful, can be shy. The like to hide and stay camouflaged and hidden from sight. If you get too close they will run off and disappear, but if you get just close enough, they will stare you down waiting for you to make a move.

standard.title: Honey Bee Lehua
standard.description: One of the most most well known flowers in Hawaiʻi, at least to the locals, is the ʻōhiʻa Lehua. In Hawaiian culture it signifies love and resilience, often found growing in barren lava fields and in higher elevation forests throughout Hawaiʻi. It also makes my personal favorite flavor of local honey.

standard.title: Stoic Chicken
standard.description: Does it get any more perfect than this rooster portrait? Usually they run away or scatter when you get close, but this time he froze and made the most majestic portrait pose I have ever seen.

standard.title: Glowing Gecko Heliconia
standard.description: The soft light shines through the canopy to illuminate a beautiful heliconia with its gecko friend standing by. This is my favorite combo to see in nature and I am always looking for geckos nearby when I see a colorful heliconia.

standard.title: Gecko Mood
standard.description: The perfect pair, a green Gold Dust Day Gecko and a Red Heliconia are the best natural compliments for each other.

standard.title: Thirsty Gecko
standard.description: A bright green day gecko takes a sip on a hot Hawaiian day. The beautiful heliconia flowers make the perfect basin to collect water and provide shelter for this colorful Hawaiian icon.

standard.title: Heliconia Heaven
standard.description: Hanging Lobster Claw Heliconia are one of the coolest plants in Hawaiʻi. Their bright reds and yellows make them stand out wherever they are. At the right time if year you can find them all flowering at the same time.

standard.title: Hanging Heliconia
standard.description: The hanging Lobster Claw Heliconia, a beautiful staple here in the Hawaiian Islands. This one standing alone, showing off its colors, while also holding a secret. If you look closely at the left side of the flower, towards the bottom, you may spot a hidden visitor.

standard.title: Hidden Gecko
standard.description: The bright red color of the heliconia and the bright green of the gecko compliment each other so naturally. A Gold Dust Day Gecko peeks out from behind a heliconia flower, looking for a drink while staying hidden and protected.

standard.title: Green and Black
standard.description: The Green and Black Dart Frog, while not native, was introduced to Hawaiʻi from Panama in hopes of helping put with mosquito control. They are hard to find, but can be seen in Mānoa valley on Oʻahu if you know where to look. Their strong green and deep brown colors are even more stunning in person.

standard.title: Lily Macro (Vertical)
standard.description: A close-up of a beautiful Purple Water Lily. The burning yellow center and the shimmering purple petals, calling for their intricate details to be admired.

standard.title: Lily Macro (Horizontal)
standard.description: Hawaiʻi is dotted with botanical gardens full of beautiful plants and animals. This Purple Water Lily just opening up shows off its burning yellow and purple center in extravagant detail.

standard.title: Lily
standard.description: The Blue Tropical Water Lily. Beautiful, detailed, colorful and sparkling with rain drops. Caught it opening up just as the sun peeked out from behind the clouds!

standard.title: Purple Perfection
standard.description: As the rain subsides and the sun comes out, a raindrop covered Purple Water Lily emerges from the pond. The amazing purple and yellow colors leave an impression on anyone that stumbles upon this hidden gem, tucked away in a Hawaiian botanical garden.

standard.title: Palm Textures
standard.description: Coconut Trees have such crazy textures and details. The deep greens and rigid lines make for pleasing visuals.

standard.title: Palm Textures II
standard.description: Coconut Trees have such crazy textures and details. They are sharp and soft while also strong and flexible.

standard.title: Coconuts
standard.description: Coconuts! The challenge is half the fun. Cutting in to a hard green coconut just to get a sip of the fresh, sweet water is an experience everyone should enjoy at least once in their life.

standard.title: Mango Perfection
standard.description: The best fruit, found abundantly on so many tropical islands around the world, the Mango. This little beauty was the last one on a lush tree in Tahiti. The bright orange exterior dotted in rain drops was calling my name!

standard.title: Cherry Blossom Camo
standard.description: Japan in the springtime is known for its wonderful cherry blossom trees or sakura. The weather is nice and things begin to warm up. They are sign of beauty and life after the cold winters. There are hundreds of varieties throughout the country, some in large groves and others scattered and hidden amongst other trees.
